visa_types.name: Study or Training, Events Or Activities
visa_types.documents_required:
Harmonized list of supporting  documents to be submitted by applicants for a Schengen visa in the Russian  Federation
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/ltu/travel-insurance)
Please note that the Mission may ask for additional documents which are not  mentioned in the list below. 
Below are the lists of documents as per the purpose of  travel jurisdiction wise:
Required documents for the purpose of travel «Study / Training /  Event / Activities» for jurisdiction of Embassy of Lithuania in Moscow
Required documents for jurisdiction of Consulate General of Lithuania in Kaliningrad (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/67AcX1ZAVYoOyyaiOQsNKy/7c3375d04913e8b1a611043fca8b9042/Required-documents-for-jurisdiction-of-Consulate-General-of-Lithuania-ENG-Aug-2025.pdf)
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Study or Training, Events Or Activities]

*Currently not applicable
VFS service fee
Apart from the visa fee, visa centres are collecting VFS service fee of the equivalent of 17,9 Euros inclusive of VAT per each application.
The service charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Persons with disabilities and persons who accompany them
Please Note: 
- Visa fee and VFS service fee can be paid at the Visa application centre in cash or by credit/debit card and are non-refundable.
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate specified by the Mission.
visa_types.processing_time:
The Embassy in Moscow shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Embassy).
The Consulate General in Kaliningrad shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Consulate).
The period for taking a decision on a visa application may be extended up to 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed.
Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy max 1 week + deadline(s) for decision making max 15 days or less if VFA) as per Art. 43(11) Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38. According to part 3 Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23, in case if additional documents should be submitted, the processing time can be extended up to 60 days.
visa_types.overview:
Overview
This site provides information on procedures applicable for those who live in Russian Federation and would like to apply for a visa to travel to Lithuania as their main country of destination in the Schengen Area.
The Consular Department of the Embassy of the Republic of Lithuania in Moscow is processing visas for the Russian citizens except those who are permanently resident in Kaliningrad area.
The Consulates General of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk are processing visas for the Russian citizens who are permanently residing in Kaliningrad area. The applicants without permanent registration in the area of jurisdiction of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk cannot be accepted.
Also the above mentioned stakeholders are issuing visas to  the following applicants:
- Third country nationals  permanently residing in Russian Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of  the stakeholder.
- Those who do not possess  citizenship of any kind or nationals of those countries where no Mission of the  Republic of Lithuania is present, and who are legally residing in Russian  Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of the stakeholder.
 If you are a citizen of Russian Federation or citizen of any other country but legally residing in Russian Federation and want to apply for a Schengen Visa, please follow the steps outlined below.
Note: Citizens of other countries, if otherwise eligible, can apply at VAC in person only. They will have to provide justification for submission the applications in Russia.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This site provides information on procedures applicable for those who live in Russian Federation and would like to apply for a visa to travel to Lithuania as their main country of destination in the Schengen Area.
The Consular Department of the Embassy of the Republic of Lithuania in Moscow is processing visas for the Russian citizens except those who are permanently resident in Kaliningrad area.
The Consulates General of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk are processing visas for the Russian citizens who are permanently residing in Kaliningrad area. The applicants without permanent registration in the area of jurisdiction of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk cannot be accepted.
Also the above mentioned stakeholders are issuing visas to  the following applicants:
- Third country nationals  permanently residing in Russian Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of  the stakeholder.
- Those who do not possess  citizenship of any kind or nationals of those countries where no Mission of the  Republic of Lithuania is present, and who are legally residing in Russian  Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of the stakeholder.
 If you are a citizen of Russian Federation or citizen of any other country but legally residing in Russian Federation and want to apply for a Schengen Visa, please follow the steps outlined below.
Note: Citizens of other countries, if otherwise eligible, can apply at VAC in person only. They will have to provide justification for submission the applications in Russia. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Study or Training, Events Or Activities]

*Currently not applicable
VFS service fee
Apart from the visa fee, visa centres are collecting VFS service fee of the equivalent of 17,9 Euros inclusive of VAT per each application.
The service charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Persons with disabilities and persons who accompany them
Please Note: 
- Visa fee and VFS service fee can be paid at the Visa application centre in cash or by credit/debit card and are non-refundable.
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate specified by the Mission.

## Documents Required
Harmonized list of supporting  documents to be submitted by applicants for a Schengen visa in the Russian  Federation
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/ltu/travel-insurance)
Please note that the Mission may ask for additional documents which are not  mentioned in the list below. 
Below are the lists of documents as per the purpose of  travel jurisdiction wise:
Required documents for the purpose of travel «Study / Training /  Event / Activities» for jurisdiction of Embassy of Lithuania in Moscow
Required documents for jurisdiction of Consulate General of Lithuania in Kaliningrad (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/67AcX1ZAVYoOyyaiOQsNKy/7c3375d04913e8b1a611043fca8b9042/Required-documents-for-jurisdiction-of-Consulate-General-of-Lithuania-ENG-Aug-2025.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for  photographs carefully before submitting your visa application.
The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face. The size of the  face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline must be between 32mm (1 ¼  inches) and 36mm (1 3/8 inches). Please look directly into the camera with a  neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be white. Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper with at least 600 dpi  resolution; the colors must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The  photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
The Embassy in Moscow shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Embassy).
The Consulate General in Kaliningrad shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Consulate).
The period for taking a decision on a visa application may be extended up to 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed.
Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy max 1 week + deadline(s) for decision making max 15 days or less if VFA) as per Art. 43(11) Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38. According to part 3 Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23, in case if additional documents should be submitted, the processing time can be extended up to 60 days.

## Download Form
Fill the application form
Schengen visa
Fill out the application and attach a copy of your passport on the MIGRIS website (https://www.migracija.lt/app/auth/login). The application must be printed and signed.
Consent  from
Dear applicants, as per the Russian Federal  Law on Personal Data Protection, all applicants who apply documents for a visa  need to ensure that the consent form is signed by each applicant and submitted  along with the application. Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2iWwmQI1hJgUQGnf3OAbz5/d409ed45b3b0029726bf00ac221c4750/newconsent.pdf) to download the consent form. Please pay attention that the form is  in Russian language only.

visa_types.name: Schengen Visa
visa_types.overview:
If you wish to visit Lithuania for a short period of time and if you come from a country with a visa requirement to enter Lithuania, you must obtain a visa prior to entry. Russian nationals require a visa to enter Lithuania. 
Applicant must choose correct Purpose of travel to Lithuania while applying for visa and the decision must be made by the applicants themselves – Visa Application Centre staff will not be able to assist in taking this decision. In case of any questions regarding the visa application the Embassy/ Consulate General may require an additional interview with the applicant. 
In case you submit the application in any of the regional centers please bear in mind the transit period between that location and Moscow. In case of incomplete documentation or in case the Embassy/ Consulate General wish to see you in person before deciding on your application, the processing time may increase. 
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Lithuania and/or the other Schengen countries* for a limited period (90 days within a period of 180 days). If you wish to reside in Lithuania for an extended period (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a National Visa. 
The possession of a visa does not confer an automatic right of entry to a Schengen country. The visa holder is requested to present proof that they fulfill the entry conditions at the border (valid travel document, valid visa, and valid purpose of travel with adequate means of travel). 
VFS is not at liberty to consider the admissibility of an application. If necessary, documents are missing, the Embassy can decide not to process the application even if VFS received and forwarded it.
Passport must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended stay in Lithuania and needs to contain at least two empty pages available for visa and/or permit sticker. A new passport must be obtained before submission of your application if the said conditions are not met. 
You can see the Visa code here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf).
* Schengen countries: Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Germany, Greece, Spain, Estonia, France, Finland, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Sweden, Switzerland, Romania, Bulgaria and Croatia.

In case you submit the application in any of the regional centers please bear in mind the transit period between that location and Moscow. In case of incomplete documentation or in case the Embassy/ Consulate General wish to see you in person before deciding on your application, the processing time may increase. 
A visa is only intended to allow a foreign national to visit Lithuania and/or the other Schengen countries* for a limited period (90 days within a period of 180 days). If you wish to reside in Lithuania for an extended period (more than 90 days), you need to apply for a National Visa.

Required documents for jurisdiction of Consulate General of Lithuania in Kaliningrad (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6vZDk63ieGaP02yqGIpwJF/a23c3b7b3803a6461837349c3dff1d84/Required-documents-for-jurisdiction-of-Consulate-General-of-Lithuania-ENG-Aug-2025.pdf)

Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/ltu/travel-insurance)
Please note that the Mission may ask for additional documents which are not  mentioned in the list below. 
Below are the lists of documents as per the purpose of  travel jurisdiction wise:
Required documents for the purpose of travel «Business» for  jurisdiction of Embassy of Lithuania in Moscow
Required documents for jurisdiction of Consulate General of Lithuania in Kaliningrad (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6vZDk63ieGaP02yqGIpwJF/a23c3b7b3803a6461837349c3dff1d84/Required-documents-for-jurisdiction-of-Consulate-General-of-Lithuania-ENG-Aug-2025.pdf)

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for  photographs carefully before submitting your visa application.
The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face. The size of the  face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline must be between 32mm (1 ¼  inches) and 36mm (1 3/8 inches). Please look directly into the camera with a  neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be white. Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper with at least 600 dpi  resolution; the colors must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The  photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
The Embassy in Moscow shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Embassy).
The Consulate General in Kaliningrad shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Consulate).
The period for taking a decision on a visa application may be extended up to 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed.
Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy max 1 week + deadline(s) for decision making max 15 days or less if VFA) as per Art. 43(11) Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38. According to part 3 Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23, in case if additional documents should be submitted, the processing time can be extended up to 60 days.

## Download Form
Fill the application form
Schengen visa
Fill out the application and attach a copy of your passport on the MIGRIS website (https://www.migracija.lt/app/auth/login). The application must be printed and signed.
Consent  from
Dear applicants, as per the Russian Federal  Law on Personal Data Protection, all applicants who apply documents for a visa  need to ensure that the consent form is signed by each applicant and submitted  along with the application. Please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2iWwmQI1hJgUQGnf3OAbz5/d409ed45b3b0029726bf00ac221c4750/newconsent.pdf) to download the consent form. Please pay attention that the form is  in Russian language only.

Harmonized list of supporting  documents to be submitted by applicants for a Schengen visa in the Russian  Federation
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/rus/en/ltu/travel-insurance)
Please note that the Mission may ask for additional documents which are not  mentioned in the list below. 
Below are the lists of documents as per the purpose of  travel jurisdiction wise:
Required documents for the purpose of travel «Tourism / Other  private visits» for jurisdiction of Embassy of Lithuania in Moscow
Required documents for jurisdiction of Consulate General of Lithuania in Kaliningrad (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1K6Zf5KtfOwd8HkiR0Tap7/26c08349749a09f6a496e03fb1649744/Required-documents-for-jurisdiction-of-Consulate-General-of-Lithuania-ENG-Aug-2025__1_.pdf)
visa_types.visa_fees:
[Table:   Tourism / Other Private Visits]

*Currently not applicable
VFS service fee
Apart from the visa fee, visa centres are collecting VFS service fee of the equivalent of 17,9 Euros inclusive of VAT per each application.
The service charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Persons with disabilities and persons who accompany them
Please Note: 
- Visa fee and VFS service fee can be paid at the Visa application centre in cash or by credit/debit card and are non-refundable.
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate specified by the Mission.

The Embassy in Moscow shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Embassy).
The Consulate General in Kaliningrad shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Consulate).
The period for taking a decision on a visa application may be extended up to 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed.
Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy max 1 week + deadline(s) for decision making max 15 days or less if VFA) as per Art. 43(11) Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38. visa_types.overview:
Overview
This site provides information on procedures applicable for those who live in Russian Federation and would like to apply for a visa to travel to Lithuania as their main country of destination in the Schengen Area.
The Consular Department of the Embassy of the Republic of Lithuania in Moscow is processing visas for the Russian citizens except those who are permanently resident in Kaliningrad area.
The Consulates General of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk are processing visas for the Russian citizens who are permanently residing in Kaliningrad area. The applicants without permanent registration in the area of jurisdiction of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk cannot be accepted.
Also the above mentioned stakeholders are issuing visas to  the following applicants:
- Third country nationals  permanently residing in Russian Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of  the stakeholder.
- Those who do not possess  citizenship of any kind or nationals of those countries where no Mission of the  Republic of Lithuania is present, and who are legally residing in Russian  Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of the stakeholder.
 If you are a citizen of Russian Federation or citizen of any other country but legally residing in Russian Federation and want to apply for a Schengen Visa, please follow the steps outlined below.
Note: Citizens of other countries, if otherwise eligible, can apply at VAC in person only. They will have to provide justification for submission the applications in Russia.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
This site provides information on procedures applicable for those who live in Russian Federation and would like to apply for a visa to travel to Lithuania as their main country of destination in the Schengen Area.
The Consular Department of the Embassy of the Republic of Lithuania in Moscow is processing visas for the Russian citizens except those who are permanently resident in Kaliningrad area.
The Consulates General of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk are processing visas for the Russian citizens who are permanently residing in Kaliningrad area. The applicants without permanent registration in the area of jurisdiction of the Republic of Lithuania in Kaliningrad and Sovetsk cannot be accepted.
Also the above mentioned stakeholders are issuing visas to  the following applicants:
- Third country nationals  permanently residing in Russian Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of  the stakeholder.
- Those who do not possess  citizenship of any kind or nationals of those countries where no Mission of the  Republic of Lithuania is present, and who are legally residing in Russian  Federation on the territory of jurisdiction of the stakeholder.
 If you are a citizen of Russian Federation or citizen of any other country but legally residing in Russian Federation and want to apply for a Schengen Visa, please follow the steps outlined below.
Note: Citizens of other countries, if otherwise eligible, can apply at VAC in person only. They will have to provide justification for submission the applications in Russia. 

## Visa Fees

[Table:   Tourism / Other Private Visits]

*Currently not applicable
VFS service fee
Apart from the visa fee, visa centres are collecting VFS service fee of the equivalent of 17,9 Euros inclusive of VAT per each application.
The service charge is waived for the following applicants:
- Children under 12 years of age
- Persons with disabilities and persons who accompany them
Please Note: 
- Visa fee and VFS service fee can be paid at the Visa application centre in cash or by credit/debit card and are non-refundable.
- The applicable visa fee in Russian Rubles is as per the current exchange rate specified by the Mission.

## Photo Specifications
Please read the specifications for  photographs carefully before submitting your visa application.
The photo needs to display a full frontal view of the face. The size of the  face from the bottom of your chin to the hairline must be between 32mm (1 ¼  inches) and 36mm (1 3/8 inches). Please look directly into the camera with a  neutral expression and without smiling.
The facial image must be sharp, clear, and with sufficient contrast. Please avoid photos with reflections or shadows on the face or with red eyes. The background should be white. Reflection from glasses, sunglasses, or tinted glasses is not permitted. The eyes cannot be hidden by the glass frames.
The photo needs to be printed on high-quality paper with at least 600 dpi  resolution; the colors must present a natural appearance and skin tone. The  photo must not have kinks, scratches or stains on it.

## Processing Time
The Embassy in Moscow shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Embassy).
The Consulate General in Kaliningrad shall take a decision on the request to issue a visa within 15 calendar days of lodging an application (application is deemed to be lodged when it is received at the Consulate).
The period for taking a decision on a visa application may be extended up to 45 calendar days in individual cases, notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed.
Time-frame of the Process (delivery to the embassy max 1 week + deadline(s) for decision making max 15 days or less if VFA) as per Art. 43(11) Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23 Visa Code; Directive 2004/38. According to part 3 Visa Code (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3HBH8SOZLLFS6GHkn5q5IK/d6a8a5270726b9e1c6107e5284936cd2/eu-visa-code-rus.pdf); Art. 23, in case if additional documents should be submitted, the processing time can be extended up to 60 days.

## Download Form
Fill the application form

Fill out the application and attach a copy of your passport on the MIGRIS website (https://www.migracija.lt/app/auth/login). The application must be printed and signed.
